good morning.example: Good morning, Mom! Did you sleep well?
good morning.when_to_use: Use before 12 PM to greet people.

good afternoon.example: Good afternoon, teacher! Are we having art class today?

good evening.example: Good evening, Dad! What's for dinner?

hello.example: Hello! My name is Maria. What is your name?

hi.example: Hi! Do you want to play with me?
hi.when_to_use: A casual and quick greeting for friends.

bye bye.example: Bye bye, puppy! Be a good dog!
bye bye.when_to_use: A cute way to say goodbye to friends and pets.

see you later.example: See you later, alligator! After a while, crocodile!
see you later.when_to_use: When you will see someone again soon.

see you tomorrow.example: See you tomorrow at school! Don't forget your homework.
see you tomorrow.when_to_use: When you will see someone the next day.

thank you.example: Thank you for the birthday present! I love it!
thank you.when_to_use: Say this when someone gives you something or helps you.

thanks.example: Thanks for helping me with my homework!
thanks.when_to_use: A casual way to say thank you.

please.example: Please, can I have some water? I am very thirsty.
please.when_to_use: Say this when you ask for something politely.

sorry.example: Sorry I stepped on your foot! I didn't mean to.
sorry.when_to_use: Say this when you make a mistake or hurt someone.

excuse me.example: Excuse me, may I pass through? Thank you!
excuse me.when_to_use: When you need to get someone's attention or pass by.

yes.example: Yes, I would love to go to the park!
yes.when_to_use: When you agree or say something is true.

no.example: No, thank you. I am not hungry right now.
no.when_to_use: When you disagree or say no to something.

okay.example: Okay, I will be ready in five minutes!
okay.when_to_use: When you agree or say everything is fine.

I love you.example: I love you, Grandma! You are the best!
I love you.when_to_use: Say this to people you care about very much.

you're welcome.example: You're welcome! I was happy to help you.
you're welcome.when_to_use: Say this when someone says thank you.

congratulations.example: Congratulations on winning the game! You did great!
congratulations.when_to_use: Say this when someone achieves something good.

good luck.example: Good luck on your test tomorrow! You will do great!
good luck.when_to_use: Say this before someone tries something important.

have fun.example: Have fun at the birthday party! Eat lots of cake!
have fun.when_to_use: Say this before someone does something fun.

be careful.example: Be careful on the stairs! Hold the railing.
be careful.when_to_use: Say this to warn someone about danger.

wait a minute.example: Wait a minute, I forgot my backpack!
wait a minute.when_to_use: When you need someone to pause for a moment.

help.example: Help! I dropped my pencil and cannot reach it!
help.when_to_use: Call this when you need assistance.
